summary: Added initial matching C for the stage switch menu module `mnstagesw`, producing 4 new function matches and data-section progress. The PR implemented stage-toggle data tables, static globals/prototypes, three internal helpers, and the exported entrypoint `mnStageSw_80237410`, while adjusting `MenuFlow.confirmed_selection` from `u32` to `u8` plus explicit padding to preserve layout.

key_files.role: New matching implementation content for the stage switch menu module, including data tables, helper functions, and the menu entrypoint.
Added include guard and declaration for `mnStageSw_80237410`.
Corrected `MenuFlow` layout by narrowing `confirmed_selection` to `u8` and adding `pad_5[3]`.

what_changed: Added `NUM_STAGES 29` and two static `u8` arrays in `mnstagesw.c`: `mnStageSw_803ED4C4`, mapping menu positions to internal stage IDs, and `mnStageSw_stageIcons`, mapping stage switch icon indices.
Added static globals `mnStageSw_804D6BF0` and `mnStageSw_804D6BF4` for the stage switch module.
Declared a block of static prototypes with address comments for remaining and newly matched `mnStageSw` functions, preserving address-oriented decomp naming.

Implemented `mnStageSw_8023593C`, which iterates over all 29 stage IDs, checks unlock state via `gm_80164430(gm_801641CC(*p))`, and syncs enable/toggle state with `gm_801641E4(*ptr, user_data[i + 2])`.
Implemented `mnStageSw_80236178`, which unhides a JObj and positions a stage icon using translation deltas between reference JOBJs, splitting the layout into indices `< 15` and `>= 15`.
Implemented `mnStageSw_802364A0`, which walks the JObj child/next chain from either `gobj->user_data` or `gobj->x34_unk` depending on whether the icon index is in the first or second column.

Implemented exported `mnStageSw_80237410`, which updates `mn_804A04F0.prev_menu`, `cur_menu`, and `hovered_selection`, creates/initializes menu GObjs, installs `fn_80235F80` as a GObj proc, and sets `proc->flags_3`.
Added `mnstagesw.h` with `MELEE_MN_STAGESW_H` guard, platform/placeholder includes, and declaration `void mnStageSw_80237410(void);`.
Changed `MenuFlow.confirmed_selection` in `mnmain.h` from `u32` to `u8` and added `u8 pad_5[3]`, indicating the real field width was one byte while the overall struct layout needed explicit padding.
smart_moves: Used explicit padding in `MenuFlow` instead of leaving an oversized `u32`, improving field accuracy while preserving downstream offsets.
Kept address comments on static prototypes and function declarations, which helps searchability and alignment with map/asm targets in future decomp work.
Used `#pragma dont_inline on/reset` around `mnStageSw_8023593C` because the matched code needed the function to remain out-of-line when called from `fn_80235F80`.
Inserted an unused `u8 pad[8]; (void) pad;` in `mnStageSw_8023593C` to force the original stack frame shape, an explicit matching tactic documented in the diff comments.
Modeled the two-column stage icon layout directly in control flow: indices below 15 use `gobj->user_data`, while indices 15 and above use `gobj->x34_unk` and subtract 15 for row positioning.
Separated menu stage IDs from icon indices using two small static `u8` tables, making data-section matching explicit and reusable.
decomp_lessons: When a struct field appears too wide for matching codegen, prefer the narrower observed type plus explicit padding; this PR changed `confirmed_selection` from `u32` to `u8` and added `pad_5[3]`.

For menu modules backed by HSD objects, `GObj` fields such as `user_data`, `user_data_remove_func`, and `x34_unk` may temporarily hold JObj references; matching code may need casts rather than invented wrapper structs until stronger evidence exists.
Stage/menu decomp often benefits from preserving raw index tables as `u8` arrays before attempting semantic enum names, especially when the table order differs from internal stage IDs.
Documenting matching-only constructs in comments, such as stack padding and non-inlining pragmas, helps future reviewers distinguish intentional codegen constraints from accidental dead code.
Address-style placeholder comments like `/// #mnStageSw_802359C8` are useful for partially decompiled modules because they mark known holes without inventing implementations.
naming_conventions: Module-prefixed names use `mnStageSw_` plus the original address, e.g. `mnStageSw_8023593C`, `mnStageSw_80236178`, and `mnStageSw_80237410`.
Local semantic names were used where evidence was clear, such as `NUM_STAGES`, `mnStageSw_stageIcons`, `jobj`, `ref_jobj`, `delta`, and `user_data`.
Unknown or less-understood functions retained generated-style names such as `fn_80235F80` and `fn_80236998` rather than receiving speculative semantic names.
Header guard follows the project path-style all-caps convention: `MELEE_MN_STAGESW_H`.
Padding field in `MenuFlow` was named `pad_5`, matching the byte offset implied by the surrounding fields.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used `#pragma dont_inline on` and `#pragma dont_inline reset` to prevent `mnStageSw_8023593C` from being inlined.
Added an unused local stack array `u8 pad[8]` with `(void) pad` to match original stack frame size.
Used `do { ... } while (i < NUM_STAGES)` in the stage-sync loop, likely to match original loop structure.
Maintained `u8` casts on `idx` and `i` in arithmetic/comparisons, e.g. `(u8) idx < 15`, to guide codegen and match byte-sized index behavior.
Used pointer iteration with both `arr` and `ptr` in `mnStageSw_8023593C` rather than simplifying to array indexing, preserving the apparent original code shape.
Matched JObj tree traversal with explicit `HSD_JObjGetChild` and repeated `HSD_JObjGetNext` loops instead of abstracting the lookup.

review_feedback.feedback: GALE01 matched code increased by 0.03% / 1256 bytes.

4 new matches were reported in `main/melee/mn/mnstagesw`: `mnStageSw_80236178` (+808 bytes), `mnStageSw_802364A0` (+168 bytes), `mnStageSw_8023593C` (+140 bytes), and `mnStageSw_80237410` (+140 bytes).
Data-section progress was reported for `mnstagesw`: `.data` +90 bytes, `.sdata2` +22 bytes, `.sdata` +16 bytes, and `.sbss` +6 bytes.
No human review comments were present in the provided PR slice.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that complete placeholder functions in `src/melee/mn/mnstagesw.c`, especially `mnStageSw_802359C8`, `mnStageSw_80235C58`, `mnStageSw_80235DC8`, `fn_80235F80`, `mnStageSw_80236548`, `fn_80236998`, and `mnStageSw_80236CBC`.
Search for other PRs changing `MenuFlow` or `mn_804A04F0` layout to confirm the `confirmed_selection` field width and padding.
Look for follow-up renames or semantic documentation of `mnStageSw_803ED4C4` and `mnStageSw_stageIcons` once stage IDs/icons are better understood.
Search for repeated use of `#pragma dont_inline` plus local stack padding in menu decomp modules as a reusable matching pattern.
